# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { open } from 'https://deno.land/x/dino@0.24.1';
// KV names will be automatically generated by denodb and can't change later on, 
export const db = await Deno.openKv({ name: "my-db" }); // you need to replace 'my-db' with your own key value store identifier for this instance of the app running in a specific environment (like dev/prod)
// Here I am assuming that we have userId and filterParam as request parameters, if they are not present it should return an error or default values. You may need to implement these parts based on how your actual application is setup 
const {userId = 'defaultUser', filterParam='defaultFilter'}  = Deno.env().toObject();   // get environment variables for user id and filters (if not present then use defaults)
// Construct the key dynamically as per requirements, this will be used to fetch data from denodb 
const keys: string[] = ['users', userId , filterParam];    // assuming that we are looking up users based on their IDs. If you're using other fields just modify these values accordingly  
// Fetch the value in KV store with corresponding key dynamically constructed above from request parameters 
const data: any =  await db?.get(keys);     // replace 'any' as per your requirement, type should be something like string or object etc. You may need to adjust this part based on what kind of response you are expecting in the KV store
